title: In Black and White
summaries: "In Black and White" by Rudyard Kipling is a collection of eight short stories published in 1888. Set in colonial India, these tales center on native Indian characters rather than British colonials. Four stories are narrated by Indians themselves, four by an English journalist observer. The collection explores themes of honor, revenge, deception, and cultural conflict through vivid encounters: a Pathan's vengeful pursuit, a missionary's humiliation, twins locked in betrayal, and miners trapped underground. Each story reveals the complex human dramas unfolding across India's diverse landscapes and communities. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
